ownership.name: Arthur Blank
ownership.style: Engaged owner who cares about culture and fan experience; willing to spend on coaching and roster
ownership.tendencies: Fired Raheem Morris after missing playoffs since 2017; brought in Stefanski and Matt Ryan (president of football) for fresh start

gm.name: Ian Cunningham
gm.tenure: 1st year (hired January 29, 2026 — formerly Bears assistant GM)
gm.philosophy: Rebuilding with limited draft capital (5 picks); must maximize value on every selection
gm.analytics_approach: Modern front office background from Bears and Eagles organizations

head_coach.name: Kevin Stefanski
head_coach.tenure: 1st year (hired January 17, 2026 — two-time AP Coach of the Year from Cleveland)
head_coach.scheme_offense: Stefanski runs a balanced offense — outside zone, play-action, RPOs; values tight ends and run game; Tua vs Penix QB battle brewing

decision_makers.primary: Ian Cunningham (GM) with Kevin Stefanski (HC) and Matt Ryan (president of football)

roster_2026.qb_situation: Tua Tagovailoa (signed 1yr/$1.3M from Dolphins) vs Michael Penix Jr. QB battle; Stefanski evaluating both
key_departures: David Onyemata (DT, left in free agency)
Darnell Mooney (WR, cut candidate for cap savings)

traded_away: Round 1 (to Rams, 2025 draft trade for James Pearce Jr.)

needs.reason: Only drafted one OL (7th round) in past two seasons; severe lack of depth; need to protect whichever QB wins the battle
23rd in yards allowed per carry (4.5); 27th in explosive run rate allowed (13.6%); lost best run defender Onyemata in free agency
Mooney's exorbitant cost makes him a likely cut; need receivers for Stefanski's offense regardless of QB
